title: Exonerated Chelsea man is suing for $1 million after 34 years in prison
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Thomas Rosa is suing the state for $1 million after his wrongful conviction and 34-year imprisonment for murder.
Rosa's lawsuit alleges Boston police misconduct, including evidence fabrication and ignoring exonerating proof.
Rosa calls for record expungement and state support, as exonerees urge lawmakers to raise compensation caps.

title: Ranking the Patriots’ 2026 schedule from least to most exciting games
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots' 2026 schedule features games against eight playoff teams from 2025, including Seahawks, Chargers, and Chiefs.
New England's schedule is challenging, with trips to Germany and the West Coast, ranking sixth hardest by 2025 opponents' win percentage.
Key matchups include a Monday Night Football game at Arrowhead against the Chiefs and an overseas game versus the Lions in Munich.

title: Lakeville police investigating pipe bomb found near town pond
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A pipe bomb was found near Egger Bog Pond by a Lakeville resident on Saturday afternoon.
The Massachusetts State Police Bomb Squad confirmed and safely disposed of the PVC pipe bomb.
Lakeville police are investigating and urge anyone with information to contact Detective Robert Schiffer.

title: Mass. McDonald’s employee to face criminal charges after viral video shows alleged food contamination
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A viral video showed a Southbridge McDonald's employee allegedly contaminating french fries before serving them to customers.
Southbridge police are filing criminal charges against the unnamed employee this week, pending arraignment in Dudley District Court.
The franchise owners confirmed those involved are no longer with McDonald's and stated local health found no public violations.

title: Boston man awaiting trial for allegedly dumping woman’s body found guilty of rape, strangulation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rinnyers Pena, 47, was found guilty of rape, strangulation, and drugging for sexual intercourse by a Boston court.
Pena faced charges from two indictments, being acquitted of kidnapping and assault but convicted on six other counts.
He is also charged with disinterring a body in connection with Alenny Matos's death and may plead guilty in that case.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 2-1 win over Minnesota United
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution beat Minnesota United 2-1 at Gillette Stadium, overcoming goals from Carles Gil and Luca Langoni.
Luca Langoni and Brayan Ceballos returned to Revolution's lineup, influencing the team's energetic start and victory.
Matt Turner made crucial saves, redeeming himself after midweek criticism, as Massachusetts fans supported him over Matt Freese.

title: SNL recap: Best moments from Will Ferrell hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’ season finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Will Ferrell hosted 'SNL' season 51 finale with Paul McCartney as the musical guest on May 16, 2026.
Ferrell portrayed Jeffrey Epstein's ghost in a cold open featuring Donald Trump, played by James Austin Johnson.
Paul McCartney performed three songs, including a new track from his upcoming album, joined by Chad Smith on drums.

title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 will occur from July 11th to 16th, featuring tall ships from over 25 countries.
Sail Boston began in 1976 and has been held in 1980, 1992, 2000, 2009, 2012, and 2017.
The 1992 and 2000 Sail Boston events faced protests due to the historical association with the slave trade.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel's presence boosts Patriots' mood at rookie minicamp after recent off-field controversy.
Despite a tumultuous offseason, Vrabel's energy on-field remains as effective as the previous season.
Newly acquired safety Kevin Byard praises Vrabel's consistent coaching approach amid 2026 season preparations.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a 426-foot two-run homer in the eighth inning to lead the Red Sox to a 3-2 win.
Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle allowed two runs in eight innings, striking out three and walking one against the Braves.
Aroldis Chapman secured the victory for Boston by recording a save despite a bases-loaded situation in the ninth inning.
